title: update openshift/api
body:
Update the openshift/api dependency to the latest commit. Several users
have reported that the go.mod couldn't be parsed, which should now be
fixed.

Fixes: #2042
Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan @nalind PTAL

comments.body:
:pushpin: Commit e9246c5 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan e9246c5c97a46c96e56cd2da3d147ec9dc59dea2 -->

@vrothberg I can confirm that this fixes the mentioned issue, at least for me:

```
olehermanse@ohfedora code $ git clone git@github.com:containers/buildah.git
Cloning into 'buildah'...
remote: Enumerating objects: 26984, done.
remote: Total 26984 (delta 0), reused 0 (delta 0), pack-reused 26984
Receiving objects: 100% (26984/26984), 22.18 MiB | 13.69 MiB/s, done.
Resolving deltas: 100% (14520/14520), done.
olehermanse@ohfedora code $ cd buildah/
olehermanse@ohfedora buildah $ go build
go: github.com/openshift/api@v3.9.1-0.20190810003144-27fb16909b15+incompatible: invalid pseudo-version: preceding tag (v3.9.0) not found
olehermanse@ohfedora buildah $ git fetch origin pull/2066/head:fix-2042

remote: Counting objects: 100% (86/86), done.

From github.com:containers/buildah
 * [new ref]           refs/pull/2066/head -> fix-2042
olehermanse@ohfedora buildah $ git checkout fix-2042
Switched to branch 'fix-2042'
olehermanse@ohfedora buildah $ go build
go: downloading github.com/containers/image/v5 v5.1.0

go: downloading github.com/containernetworking/cni v0.7.1

```
